title: fix: invalidate KV cache when API key permissions change
author: ElliotEtag

gist.headline: Fresh keys, fresh cache
gist.blurb: Fixed a sneaky bug where updating API key permissions wouldn't immediately take effect. Now, when you restrict a key to specific models, the hive instantly respects your boundaries.
gist.summary: Fixed an issue where updating an API key's allowed models did not invalidate the KV cache, causing the API to serve stale permission data.
gist.impact: Developers will now see API key permission changes take effect immediately, ensuring model restrictions are strictly enforced.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a security guard with a new key, while Nomnom eats the old stale cache data. Represents refreshing permissions and clearing out the old cache.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny security guard hat holding a shiny golden key, while the round Nomnom creature happily eats a dusty box labeled 'CACHE'. Background features cozy server racks glowing with soft lime green (#ecf874) lights. Soft pastel gradient background, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
